title: Pesticides and Cancer: A New Study
summary: A new study found that pesticides, which are chemicals used to kill bugs on farms, might increase the risk of getting cancer. Scientists looked at places in Peru where many pesticides are used. They found that people living near farms had higher chances of getting cancer. The study showed that people in these areas were 150% more likely to get cancer. Pesticides can get into our food, water, and air. Even though each pesticide alone might not be very dangerous, being around many different pesticides together can be harmful. The scientists also found that pesticides can cause changes in our bodies even before we get sick. This study is important because it helps us understand how to keep people safe from harmful chemicals.

background_read: Pesticides are chemicals that farmers use to protect their crops from insects and other pests. These chemicals can stay on fruits and vegetables and also get into soil and water. When people eat food or drink water with pesticides, the chemicals can enter their bodies. Some studies have suggested that being around many pesticides for a long time might be bad for health. This new study looked at many people in Peru and found a strong link between pesticides and cancer. The study is important because it helps us learn how to reduce our exposure to harmful chemicals.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists from France and Peru studied people in Peru.
WHAT: They found that pesticides are linked to a 150% higher risk of cancer.
WHERE: The study took place in Peru, especially in farming areas.
WHY: To understand how pesticides affect health and help keep people safe.

perspectives.description: They believe the study shows a clear link between pesticides and cancer.
They may worry about their health and want safer ways to farm.
